points.text: Common symptoms include coughing, fever, and loss of smell and taste.
Symptoms may also include headaches, nasal congestion, and muscle pain.
Breathing difficulties can occur in moderate to severe cases.
Fever, fatigue, cough, and breathing difficulties are often reported.
Loss of taste combined with loss of smell is reported in many cases.
A respiratory symptom cluster includes cough, sputum, and shortness of breath.
Muscle and joint pain, headache, and fatigue are part of a musculoskeletal symptom cluster.
Digestive symptoms can include abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea.
COVID-19 symptoms often show up 2 to 14 days after contact with the virus.
Common symptoms include dry cough, shortness of breath, and loss of taste or smell.
Extreme tiredness, called fatigue, is also a common symptom of COVID-19.
Digestive symptoms such as upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea can occur.
Pain, including headaches and body or muscle aches, is a symptom of COVID-19.
Fever or chills are common symptoms associated with COVID-19.
Dizziness and dry mouth can also be symptoms of COVID-19.
New confusion and trouble staying awake are serious symptoms to watch for.
Chest pain or pressure that is constant requires emergency help.
Symptoms may worsen about 5 to 7 days after they start.
The most common symptoms are fever, cough, and tiredness.
Other symptoms can include shortness of breath and chest pain or pressure.
Muscle or body aches, headache, and loss of taste or smell are also common.
Confusion, sore throat, and congestion or runny nose may occur.
Diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, abdominal pain, and skin rashes can be symptoms.
Infants may experience difficulty feeding as a symptom of COVID-19.
